meta.purpose: Demonstrate communication patterns through concrete cases showing structure, not scripts.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks for examples, demonstrations, or full cases.

cases.title: Repairing after a misunderstood message
cases.context: A team lead sent a message about a missed deadline. The recipient interpreted it as blame and became defensive. The lead's intent was to understand blockers and help.
cases.what_happened: Instead of explaining the original message, the lead acknowledged the recipient's reaction first: 'I can see my message landed as criticism. That wasn't my intent. Can we reset and focus on what blocked the work?'
cases.what_was_learned: When a message triggers a defensive reaction, further explanation of the original message deepens the conflict. The first move is state repair, not content clarification.

cases.title: Polished words with unclear intent still fail
cases.context: A manager spent an hour crafting diplomatic language to tell a direct report their performance needed improvement. The message was polite and carefully worded.
cases.what_happened: The direct report heard the message as a generic HR warning. They asked 'are you firing me?' because the polite language obscured the actual intent: the manager wanted to offer support and a concrete improvement plan.
cases.what_was_learned: Politeness without intent clarity creates ambiguity. Starting with 'I want to support your growth and I have concrete observations about where we can improve' changes what the listener hears.
